events.title: God creates the heavens and the earth
events.summary: By his word alone, God brings light, order, and life out of nothing — the foundation every later story builds on.

events.title: Joseph forgives his brothers in Egypt
events.summary: Sold into slavery, raised to power, Joseph names the pattern of providence: what others meant for evil, God meant for good.

events.title: Joshua commissioned to lead Israel
events.summary: After Moses, Joshua receives the charge that echoes through Scripture: be strong and courageous, for the LORD goes with you.

events.title: David defeats Goliath
events.summary: A shepherd boy with a sling meets a giant with a spear — and reframes the battle: it belongs to the LORD.

events.title: David composes the shepherd psalms
events.summary: The warrior-king writes as a sheep: the LORD is my shepherd. Israel's songbook takes shape.

events.title: Isaiah speaks comfort to a fearful people
events.summary: Facing empires and exile, the prophet carries God's refrain: fear not, for I am with you.

events.title: Jeremiah's letter to the exiles
events.summary: To a displaced generation in Babylon: God's plans hold a future and a hope — even in a land you didn't choose.

events.title: The Word becomes flesh
events.summary: John reaches back to Genesis — in the beginning was the Word — and announces that the Word has moved into the neighborhood.

events.title: The Sermon on the Mount
events.summary: On a Galilean hillside, Jesus dismantles worry: look at the birds, consider the lilies, seek first the kingdom.

events.title: Peace promised in the upper room
events.summary: On the night of his arrest, Jesus leaves his friends an inheritance no one can confiscate: my peace I give unto you.

events.title: Paul writes to the Romans
events.summary: From Corinth, Paul writes his fullest account of the gospel — and its most quoted assurance: all things work together for good.

events.title: Paul writes from prison in Rome
events.summary: In chains, Paul writes the New Testament's most joyful letter: rejoice, be anxious for nothing, and the peace of God will guard you.

events.title: The letter to the Hebrews defines faith
events.summary: Faith is the substance of things hoped for — then a roll call of everyone from Abel to David who lived it.
